content.full_text:
87206

A-99-
301

October 12, 1977


Candance J. Busby
Deputy County Clerk
Imperial County
Courthouse
El Centro, California  92243

Dear Ms. Busby:

     Thank you for your letter of September 13, 1977. Planning
commissioners file on a Form 716 in April under the Government
Conflict of Interests Act (authored by Moscone, not Waxman-Dymally)
until their agency's conflict of interest code has been approved
by the code reviewing body. After the code has been approved,
planning commissioners will file a designated form pursuant to the
provisions of their code.

     I hope the above information answers your question. Please
let me know if I may be of further assistance.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Mary Ann Kvasager
                                        Technical Assistance & Analysis
                                        Division

MAK:bw

HARRY M. FREE
County Clerk

MARGARET I. MOTT
Assistant County Clerk

Courthouse
El Centro 92243
Tele: (714) 352-3610, Ext.227

September 13,1977

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

TO: Technical Assistance and Analysis Division

RE: Statement of Economic Interests

Gentlemen:

The March 16,1977 memo from your office dealing with Statement of

Economic Interests, Planning Commissions (Page 2,V.) specifies the require-

ments for filing form 716. Has there been a memo subsequent to this one?

If so please send a copy to me. I assumed this position in July and have

been trying to locate all of your memos and to familiarize myself with the

regulations. It appears to me that I do not have all of your memos on this

subject.

It has been my understanding that the Planning Commission members and

officers were to file according to the Waxman-Dymally Act for the last time

in April of this year on forms 716; and that after that time they were to

file according to the Conflict of Interest Code for their agency. That if

no code had been approved for their agency they were to continue to file

under Waxman-Dymally, but on form 720, as it had replaced form 716.

I have been approached by a new member of the Planning Commission of

the city of Calexico for forms to file. Their agency is not included under

the Conflict of Interest Code for the Imperial County Planning Department.

Page 2
Sept. 13, 1977


The City Clerk's Office in Calexico tells me that the members are to file

form 716. Please send me clarifying information. Do Planning Commission

members and officers file form 716 or 720?


Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

HARRY M. FREE, COUNTY CLERK
BY,

[signature]
CANDACE J. BUSBY, DEPUTY

sections.question: Do Planning Commission members and officers file form 716 or 720? The inquiry concerns the correct filing form for planning commissioners when their agency's conflict of interest code has not yet been approved.
sections.conclusion: Planning commissioners file on a Form 716 in April under the Governmental Conflict of Interests Act (authored by Moscone, not Waxman-Dymally) until their agency's conflict of interest code has been approved by the code reviewing body. After the code has been approved, planning commissioners will file a designated form pursuant to the provisions of their code.
sections.question_synthetic: The Deputy County Clerk seeks clarification on whether planning commissioners file Form 716 or Form 720 when their agency lacks an approved conflict of interest code.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Planning commissioners must file Form 716 under the Moscone Act until their agency's conflict of interest code is approved, after which they file the designated form specified in their code.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from Candance J. Busby/Harry M. Free) and FPPC response (from Mary Ann Kvasager). Response section is brief and lacks formal CONCLUSION heading. Incoming request text is complete and clearly describes the Q&A structure.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do Planning Commission members and officers file form 716 or 720? The inquiry concerns the correct filing form for planning commissioners when their agency's conflict of interest code has not yet been approved.

CONCLUSION: Planning commissioners file on a Form 716 in April under the Governmental Conflict of Interests Act (authored by Moscone, not Waxman-Dymally) until their agency's conflict of interest code has been approved by the code reviewing body. After the code has been approved, planning commissioners will file a designated form pursuant to the provisions of their code.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to Imperial County Clerk's inquiry about the appropriate filing form for planning commissioners, clarifying that Form 716 is required until an agency's conflict of interest code is approved.
